Marketing

Is a Psychology Today profile worth it for therapists in 2025?

Last updated: January 15, 2025 • 5 min read

Is a Psychology Today profile worth it for therapists in 2025?

You’ve opened your solo practice, the website is live, and… crickets. You keep hearing colleagues say, “Just get on Psychology Today,” but you’re already paying for EHR, liability insurance, and that downtown office you use twice a week. Will another monthly fee actually bring clients, or is it money down the drain?

Below I unpack the real numbers—pricing, clicks, conversion rates, and the hidden costs—so you can decide if a Psychology Today profile is worth it for your practice in 2025.

Quick Verdict
For most new solo practices, Psychology Today is still the fastest way to show up on Google’s page 1, but you’ll need to re-evaluate every six months once your wait-list is stable. If you already rank organically or your niche is tiny, skip it and lean on free directories instead.

What you’re paying in 2025

CountryBasic MonthlyFeatured MonthlyContract
US$29.95+$99Cancel any time
UK£24.95+£79Cancel any time
AUAU $39.95+AU $119Cancel any time
CACA $34.95+CA $99Cancel any time

There’s no set-up fee and you can ask for a pro-rata refund when you cancel.

Core features (and what’s missing)

  • Public-facing directory with telehealth, insurance, and identity filters added 2023-24
  • SEO weight: >70% of “therapist + city” searches still return a PT link on page 1
  • Lead export: copy-paste only—no API or direct plug-ins to SimplePractice, TheraNest, or other EHRs
  • Optional video link via Telehealth by SimplePractice (added 2024)

Pros that matter

  • Instant Google presence without a blog
  • Zero tech set-up—fill boxes, hit publish
  • Cross-state/province telehealth traffic (where legal)
  • Built-in trust factor for therapy-seekers

Cons that annoy

  • High competition: 300+ profiles in large metros
  • No analytics beyond raw clicks
  • No booking, no payment, no auto-follow-up
  • Directory shows clients your competitors too
  • Basic profiles get buried unless you pay for Featured

Best for

  • New practices (<12 months) that need clients now
  • Niche clinicians (e.g., EMDR, ADHD adults, culturally-specific tags)
  • Therapists who hate SEO and don’t yet rank organically

Head-to-head: Psychology Today vs. the main alternatives

FeaturePsychology TodayTherapyDenGoogle Business Profile
Monthly cost~$30–$130FreeFree
Traffic volumeHighMediumHigh (if you rank)
SEO helpImmediateSomeDepends on your site
Ethical pay-to-playNoYesN/A
Integrated bookingNoNoVia website
AnalyticsClicks onlyClicks onlyFull Google stats
ContractMonth-to-monthMonth-to-monthN/A

Who should choose what

Pick Psychology Today if

  • You’re starting out and need clients fast
  • Your niche is underserved in your postcode
  • Monthly client value is ≥ $120 (US/CA), £80 (UK), AU $150 (AU)—one client covers the annual fee

Skip it and pick TherapyDen or Google Business Profile if

  • You already rank on page 1 for “therapist + city”
  • You’re full with a wait-list
  • You object to pay-to-play directories or prefer free ethical options

FAQ

Is a Psychology Today featured listing worth the extra cost in 2025?

Only if you’re in a competitive metro area and can recoup the fee with one new monthly client; otherwise stay basic and invest the savings in SEO.

How many clients actually come from a standard Psychology Today profile?

2024 data: 3.2 paying clients per 100 clicks. Expect 6–12 clicks/month in large cities, 2–4 in rural postcodes.

What are the best alternatives to Psychology Today for therapists?

TherapyDen (free), Google Business Profile (free), BACP/UKCP directories in the UK, Headway if you’re insurance-panelled in the US.

Does Psychology Today integrate with SimplePractice or other EHRs?

No—leads must be exported manually or copy-pasted into your practice-management software.

How often should I update my Psychology Today profile to stay visible?

Refresh your photo every 12 months and tweak narrative tags every 3–6 months; the algorithm rewards “fresh” profiles.

Bottom line

A Psychology Today profile is still the quickest short-cut to Google visibility for most solo therapists in 2025, but treat it like a faucet—turn it on when you need clients, off when you don’t. Combine it with a free Google Business Profile and an ethical no-pay directory so you’re not locked into monthly fees forever.

Ready to test it? You can open (or cancel) a profile here: Try PSYCHOLOGY_TODAY free →.
